summary: @sqlite-node/createsql ships an obfuscated dropper that fetches and executes remote code on require()
details: Package self-describes as 'A SQLite Toolkit' but contains no SQLite code. The single shipped file index.js is obfuscator.io-packed (244-entry RC4+base64 rotated string array, control-flow flattening, hex-named identifiers). On module load it invokes child_process.exec with {windowsHide:true, cwd:process.cwd()}, performs an HTTPS GET to a runtime-decoded URL, writes the response body to a file in the consumer's current working directory with flag 'w+', and exec's the resulting payload. The cover-story description, typosquat-style scope (@sqlite-node), heavy obfuscation, and remote-fetch-write-exec pipeline together match the canonical install/load-time dropper shape. require()'ing this package executes attacker-controlled bytes on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
